title: OD #1 — Narcan x2
description: Fentanyl overdose, unresponsive. EMS administered 2x Narcan. Transported to Catawba Valley Medical Center ED. Refused further treatment, AMA discharge.
location: Residence — 212 Main Ave NW, Hickory, NC

title: Detox Admission — CVMC
description: Self-admitted to Catawba Valley Medical Center detox program. 7-day stay. Referred to MAT program on discharge.

title: Depression Screening — PHQ-9 Score: 16
description: Initial mental health assessment. PHQ-9 score 16 (Moderately Severe Depression). Referred to behavioral health counseling. Not initiated due to waitlist.

title: Hepatitis C Diagnosis
description: Positive HCV antibody test. Genotype 1a confirmed. Liver enzymes elevated. Referred to hepatology, appointment scheduled for 6 weeks.

title: Eviction — Lost Housing
description: Evicted from rental property at 212 Main Ave NW, Hickory. Cited non-payment of rent. Entered homeless status. Applied to Hickory Rescue Mission, 45-day waitlist.

title: OD #2 — Narcan + Benzos
description: Fentanyl + benzodiazepine overdose. Found unresponsive under 2nd St bridge. EMS administered Narcan x3 — slow reversal due to benzo involvement. Transported to CVMC. 2-day observation.

title: MAT Initiated — Suboxone 16mg
description: Buprenorphine/naloxone (Suboxone 16mg) initiated at Catawba Recovery Center. Engaged with peer specialist Tanya Williams. Stable for first 6 weeks.

title: Entered Emergency Shelter
description: Admitted to Hickory Rescue Mission emergency shelter. Stable housing secured after 2-month wait. Case manager assigned.

title: MAT Discontinued — Patient Request
description: Marcus stopped Suboxone without taper. Cited side effects and desire to be 'completely clean.' Risk of relapse elevated significantly. Peer specialist notified.

title: Arrested — Possession Charge
description: Arrested on drug possession charge (Schedule I). Remanded to Catawba County Detention Center pending trial. Bond set at $5,000.

title: Mental Health Crisis — Incarcerated
description: Reported suicidal ideation to jail medical staff. Placed on mental health watch. Psychiatrist consult. Started on antidepressant.

title: OD #3 — Released from Custody (Day 1)
description: Overdosed less than 24 hours after release from CCDC. Fentanyl, significantly reduced tolerance post-incarceration. EMS + Narcan. Pattern consistent with incarceration release risk.
location: Near Catawba County Detention Center, Hickory, NC

title: MAT Re-initiated — Suboxone 8mg
description: Re-enrolled in MAT program after post-release OD. Lower initial dose 8mg due to post-incarceration tolerance changes. Weekly check-ins scheduled.

title: Mental Health Follow-up — Stable
description: Follow-up psychiatric evaluation. Depression symptoms improved on medication. PHQ-9 score 9. Continued on antidepressant. No suicidal ideation reported.

title: MAT Stopped — Lost Insurance Coverage
description: Suboxone prescription lapsed when Medicaid coverage disrupted after address change. Unable to get prior auth in time. 3-week gap in coverage.

title: Re-Arrested — Violation of Probation
description: Arrested on probation violation. Remanded to CCDC for 45-day sentence.

title: Released from Custody — High Risk Window
description: Released from CCDC after 45-day sentence. No MAT bridge prescription provided at release. No naloxone kit. Returned to emergency shelter. HIGH RISK: post-incarceration OD window.

title: Elevated Liver Enzymes — Hep C Progression
description: Lab results: ALT 145 U/L (normal <56), AST 98 U/L. Liver disease progression concerning. Hep C treatment still not initiated. Hepatology referral renewed.

title: OD #4 — TODAY (Narcan administered)
description: Fentanyl + benzodiazepine overdose. Found unresponsive at Hickory Rescue Mission. Staff administered Narcan. EMS dispatched. Transported to Catawba Valley Medical Center ED. DART assessment triggered.

title: OD #1
description: Fentanyl overdose at residence in Hickory, NC. EMS responded.

title: OD #2
description: Fentanyl + meth overdose. Narcan administered. Transported to CVMC.

title: ED Visit Post-OD — Declined MAT
description: Post-OD evaluation at CVMC ED. Medically stable. Patient declined MAT referral. Discharged AMA. Peer outreach scheduled.

title: OD #1 — Fentanyl
description: Fentanyl overdose at residence. Roommate called 911. Narcan administered. Transported to CVMC ED. Pregnancy not yet known.

title: MAT Initiated — Subutex 8mg
description: Buprenorphine mono-product (Subutex) initiated — preferred for reproductive-age women. Stable for 6 weeks then lost to follow-up.

title: MAT Discontinued — Lost to Follow-Up
description: Ashley missed 3 consecutive appointments. Subutex prescription lapsed. Outreach attempted x2, no response.

title: OD #2 — Fentanyl + Meth
description: Poly-substance overdose in a vehicle behind 1st Ave, Hickory. Bystander called 911. Narcan administered x2. Transported to CVMC. Discharged next day.

title: Pregnancy Confirmed — ~8 weeks
description: Positive pregnancy test at Catawba Co. Public Health. Estimated 8 weeks gestation. Referred to OB/GYN and high-risk pregnancy program. SUD and pregnancy case conference opened.

title: Mental Health Assessment — Prenatal
description: Edinburgh Postnatal Depression Scale (antenatal): Score 18 (high risk). Referred to perinatal mental health services. Waitlist 3 weeks.

title: OD #3 — PREGNANT — TODAY
description: Fentanyl + meth overdose at 14 weeks gestation. Found unresponsive. Narcan administered x2. Fetal monitoring en route. Transported to CVMC OB emergency. DART alert triggered. Dual patient status: mother + fetus.

title: VA PTSD Assessment
description: PCL-5 score 52 (moderate PTSD). Two combat deployments (Iraq 2004, Afghanistan 2009). Chronic pain from shrapnel injury. Referred to SUD treatment for co-occurring alcohol/opioid use.

title: Near-OD — Alcohol + Opioids
description: Family called 911 after Raymond was unresponsive. Alcohol + Rx opioids (hydrocodone). Naloxone not required — primarily alcohol. Transported to CVMC, 1-day observation.

title: Vivitrol Initiated — Naltrexone IM
description: Naltrexone monthly injection (Vivitrol) initiated. Blocks opioid receptors and reduces alcohol craving. VA-eligible medication. Monthly injection scheduled at Catawba Recovery Center.

title: Vivitrol Month 2 — Stable
description: Second monthly injection administered. Patient reports no alcohol use in 30 days. Pain management plan revised with non-opioid analgesics. PTSD counseling engaged.

title: PTSD Follow-up — Improving
description: PCL-5 score 34 (mild). Significant improvement from 52 at intake. Prolonged Exposure therapy initiated. Employment counseling referral made.

title: Vivitrol Month 5 — Stable
description: 5th monthly injection administered. Patient remains abstinent from alcohol and opioids. Returned to part-time work. Family engagement noted. Progressing well.

title: OD #5 — Fentanyl + Xylazine
description: Fentanyl + xylazine (tranq) overdose. Multiple Narcan doses with delayed reversal due to xylazine. Transported to CVMC ED.

title: EMS Call — Poly-substance OD
description: Poly-substance overdose, unresponsive near 1st St SW overpass. Fentanyl + meth + benzos. 3rd OD this year. Narcan administered x3.

title: OD #2 — Fentanyl
description: OD #2 — Fentanyl. Parents called 911. Narcan administered by first responders. Transported to CVMC.

title: Released from CCDC — High Risk Window
description: Released from Catawba County Detention Center after 60-day sentence. No MAT bridge. No naloxone issued. High-risk post-incarceration window.

title: Street Outreach Contact
description: Outreach worker contacted Kevin near downtown shelter. Naloxone kit distributed. Declined MAT referral. Will consider peer support.

title: ED Visit — Meth + Fentanyl Intoxication
description: Brought to CVMC ED by bystander. Agitated, paranoid. Meth + fentanyl detected on UDS. Observed 8 hours, medically cleared. DART score updated.
